summary: Large Great Bay stage decompilation pass centered on src/melee/gr/grgreatbay.c, replacing several nonmatching stubs with C implementations and tightening related ground/item types. The decomp.dev report recorded 4 new full matches in grgreatbay, major near-matches for additional functions, and a small .sdata regression.
what_changed: Implemented multiple Great Bay functions that were previously documented as nonmatching stubs, including grGreatBay_801F4694, grGreatBay_801F499C, grGreatBay_801F5460, grGreatBay_801F5600, grGreatBay_801F5AF8, grGreatBay_801F5E28, grGreatBay_801F60C4, and grGreatBay_801F63F4.

Added a local grGb_StageAttr struct in grgreatbay.c and changed grGb_804D69E0_t.x0 from s16* to grGb_StageAttr*, replacing raw indexed stage-attribute access with named offset fields such as x44, x46, x68, x70, x78, and x7C.

Expanded Great Bay ground variable layouts in src/melee/gr/types.h: added bitfields for greatbay.xC5, generator/timer/position fields, converted four separate greatbay2 child gobj members into gobjs[4], added greatbay2 bitflags, extended greatbay3, and added grGreatBay_GroundVars4 for the Tingle-related object state.
Added shared rand_range and ZRANDI helpers to src/melee/gr/inlines.h, then replaced repeated local rand_inline implementations in grizumi.c, grkraid.c, groldkongo.c, and groldyoshi.c.
Updated grGreatBay_801F63F4's declaration from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to bool grGreatBay_801F63F4(Ground_GObj*) in grgreatbay.h.
Updated Tingle item prototypes in ittincle.h, including it_802EBFAC(Item_GObj*) and it_802ECA70(HSD_GObj*) returning Item_GObj*, enabling typed calls from the Great Bay logic.
Added dependencies in grgreatbay.c for stage/item behavior: gr/stage.h, it/it_266F.h, it/item.h, and it/items/ittincle.h.
Bot-reported match impact: +2816 bytes matched code, 4 new full matches, and 6 improvements in unmatched grgreatbay items; bot also reported .sdata dropping from 100.00% to 81.82%.
smart_moves: Recovered a structured stage-attribute table before using it heavily; this made later Great Bay functions read as field accesses instead of fragile s16-index arithmetic.
Factored the repeated random-range idiom into rand_range/ZRANDI, preserving the original zero-safe HSD_Randi behavior while removing duplicated inline functions across several stage files.

Modeled packed state with explicit bitfields, such as greatbay.xC5.b0123456 and b7 and greatbay2.x12.b0 through b3, which aligns the C layout with observed flag usage without inventing semantic names prematurely.
Replaced gobj5/gobj6/gobj7/gobj8 with gobjs[4], matching the looped child-object handling in grGreatBay_801F5AF8 and reducing duplicate code.

Typed cross-system item calls only as far as the diff supported: Tingle function signatures were upgraded enough for Great Bay to create/control an Item_GObj without overnaming the unknown Tingle motions.

Used existing ground/library primitives consistently: GET_GROUND, GET_JOBJ, Ground_801C3FA4, grAnime_* helpers, mpLib/mpJointList operations, effects via grLib_801C96F8/801C97DC, and sound via Ground_801C53EC.
decomp_lessons: When a stage has a large data-driven behavior table, introduce a temporary offset-named struct before trying to infer semantic field names; it improves matching and keeps the evidence trail clear.
A shared inline helper can be a matching tactic: replacing per-file local inline random-range functions with one in gr/inlines.h avoided repetition while preserving generated behavior.
Arrays are preferable to numbered fields when the assembly iterates over adjacent objects; the greatbay2.gobjs[4] refactor is a useful pattern for child ground objects.
Use bitfields to document packed state only when the diff shows exact flag-level access; this PR kept placeholder names but captured the layout needed for matching.
Header cleanups often unblock decompilation: changing UNK prototypes to typed signatures in grgreatbay.h and ittincle.h allowed newly decompiled code to call item/stage helpers directly.
Bot match reports should be checked for data regressions as well as code gains; this PR gained significant code matching but broke a small .sdata match.
naming_conventions: Stage-local recovered structs used the grGb_ prefix, e.g. grGb_StageAttr and grGb_804D69E0_t.
Unknown-but-offset-known fields retained x-offset names such as xC4, xC5, xC6, xC8, xCC, x70, x78, and x7C.
Bitfield members used bit-position names such as b0123456, b7, b0, b1, b2, b3, and b4567.
The shared random helper was named rand_range, replacing local rand_inline helpers in multiple ground files.
Child object fields were generalized from numbered names gobj5 through gobj8 to an indexed gobjs[4] array.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK annotations in several functions to satisfy stack/layout requirements where needed.
Preserved zero-safe random generation with ZRANDI(n) expanding to HSD_Randi(n) only when n is nonzero.
Inserted no-op-looking expression current += 0 in grGreatBay_801F62F8, likely to influence codegen while keeping behavior unchanged.
Kept explicit HSD_ASSERT calls at line-number-like constants in state-machine error paths, including unreachable Great Bay switch cases.
Maintained local temporaries such as timer, max_val, min_val, factor, and nested blocks around clamp logic to shape generated code.
Used exact float constants visible in the diff, including 1.5707964f for ±90-degree rotation and 0.017453292f for degree-to-radian scaling.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
decomp.dev bot reported 4 new full matches: grGreatBay_801F5600 (+788 bytes), grGreatBay_801F4694 (+768 bytes), grGreatBay_801F5E28 (+668 bytes), and grGreatBay_801F5AF8 (+592 bytes).

decomp.dev bot reported major improvements but not full matches for grGreatBay_801F499C at 99.90%, grGreatBay_801F60C4 at 94.51%, grGreatBay_801F63F4 at 96.63%, grGreatBay_801F5460 at 97.30%, .sdata2 at 87.50%, and grGreatBay_801F62F8 at 99.92%.
decomp.dev bot reported one broken match: main/melee/gr/grgreatbay .sdata decreased from 100.00% to 81.82% (-5 bytes).
follow_up_queries: Check later PRs that recover semantic names for grGb_StageAttr fields x0 through x7C.
Find follow-up work that restores the grgreatbay .sdata match after this PR's -5 byte regression.
Search for later full matches of grGreatBay_801F499C, grGreatBay_801F60C4, grGreatBay_801F63F4, and grGreatBay_801F5460.
Search for additional uses or refinements of rand_range and ZRANDI in ground stage files.
Check whether Tingle item functions it_802ECA70, it_802EBFAC, it_802EBD14, it_802ECC98, and it_802ECCA4 were renamed or further typed later.
